reforms.description: The Korean government is significantly increasing its investment in R&D to support the production of essential raw materials for the semiconductor industry and to enhance support for innovative SMEs through a graduation scheme for technology credit guarantees. The budget for R&D related to carbon neutrality has been more than doubled to KRW 1.9 trillion in 2022, reflecting a commitment to fostering innovation and growth in key industries.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The increased funding for R&D and support for innovative SMEs are expected to enhance productivity and innovation in critical sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The budget for R&D related to carbon neutrality has been confirmed for 2022, and the graduation scheme for technology credit guarantees has been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a structural change in R&D funding and SME support, significantly impacting the innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: The government is also investing in R&D to homeshore the production of other essential raw materials such as krypton and xenon.

reforms.description: The government introduced an evaluation scheme, the SMEs Integrated Management System, to track SME central government support programmes to enhance their effectiveness.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The evaluation scheme aims to improve the effectiveness of support programmes, which can lead to better outcomes for SMEs.
reforms.status_evidence: The evaluation scheme has been officially introduced and is in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a new evaluation scheme represents a significant improvement in the governance of SME support.
reforms.source_quote: The government introduced an evaluation scheme, the SMEs Integrated Management System, to track SME central government support programmes.

reforms.description: The Korean government plans to reform the SME policy evaluation system to focus more on boosting innovation and growth potential of SMEs, moving away from excessive support for low-performing firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By shifting focus to high-potential firms, the reform aims to enhance the overall innovation capacity of the SME sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has officially announced plans for the reform.
reforms.importance_rationale: The planned reform represents a significant shift in policy direction for SME support.
reforms.source_quote: The new government plans to reform the SME policy evaluation system to more focus on boosting innovation and growth potential of SMEs.

reforms.description: The government has launched regulatory sandboxes to identify excessive regulation and facilitate innovation in various sectors, including ICT and R&D.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing regulatory burdens, the initiative aims to foster innovation and support new business models.
reforms.status_evidence: The regulatory sandbox initiative has been officially launched and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of regulatory sandboxes represents a significant effort to improve the innovation environment.
reforms.source_quote: The government has launched regulatory sandboxes in ICT convergence, industrial convergence, financial innovation, regional innovation, smart cities and R&D.
